zh.lifeSummary: 當Baron Richard Beauchamp於1453年出生在英國維爾爾時，他的父親William de Beauchamp46歲，母親Elizabeth de Braybrook44歲。他與Ann Cartwright結婚。他去世於1508年6月12日在西班牙享年55歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭倫敦盧蓋特。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Richard
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the most enduringly successful of the Old French personal names introduced into Britain by the Normans. It is of Germanic (Frankish) origin, derived from rīc ‘power’ + hard ‘strong, hardy’. It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

variants.name: Rikert

It has enjoyed continuous popularity in England from the Conquest to the present day, influenced by the fact that it was borne by three kings of England, in particular Richard I ( 1157–99 ). He was king for only ten years ( 1189–99 ), most of which he spent in warfare abroad, taking part in the Third Crusade and costing the people of England considerable sums in taxes. Nevertheless, he achieved the status of a folk hero, and was never in England long enough to disappoint popular faith in his goodness and justice. He was also Duke of Aquitaine and Normandy and Count of Anjou, fiefs which he held at a time of maximum English expansion in France. His exploits as a leader of the Third Crusade earned him the nickname ‘Coeur de Lion’ or ‘Lionheart’ and a permanent place in popular imagination, in which he was even more firmly enshrined by Sir Walter Scott's novel Ivanhoe ( 1820 ).

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.
The marriage of Prince Ferdinand of Aragon and Princess Isabella of Castille.
Christopher Columbus voyage to America was financed by Queen Isabella I and her husband, Ferdinand II of Aragon.
The Muslims of Granada were defeated.

en.lifeSummary: When Baron Richard Beauchamp was born in 1453, in Wiltshire, England, his father, William de Beauchamp, was 46 and his mother, Elizabeth de Braybrook, was 44. He married Ann Cartwright. He died on 12 June 1508, in Spain, at the age of 55, and was buried in Ludgate, London, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Baron Richard Beauchamp est né en 1453, à Wiltshire, Angleterre, son père, William de Beauchamp, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth de Braybrook, avait 44 ans. Il a épousé Ann Cartwright. Il est décédé le 12 juin 1508, à Espagne, à l’âge de 55 ans, et a été enterré à Ludgate, Londres, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Baron Richard Beauchamp 1453 in Wiltshire, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater William de Beauchamp 46 und seine Mutter Elizabeth de Braybrook 44. Er hat Ann Cartwright geheiratet. Er starb am 12. Juni 1508 im Alter von 55 Jahren in Spanien und wurde in Ludgate, London, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Baron Richard Beauchamp è nato il nel 1453 nel Wiltshire, England, il suo padre, William de Beauchamp, aveva 46 anni e il suo madre, Elizabeth de Braybrook, aveva 44 anni. Lui sposato Ann Cartwright. Morì il 12 giugno 1508, nel Spagna, all'età di 55 anni, e fu sepolto a Ludgate, London, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ko.lifeSummary: Baron Richard Beauchamp(이)가 1453년 영국 윌츠히르에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 William de Beauchamp(은)는 46세였고, 그의 어머니 Elizabeth de Braybrook(은)는 44세였다. 그는 Ann Cartwright(와)과 결혼했다. 그는 1508년 6월 12일 스페인에서 55세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 런던 루드가트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Baron Richard Beauchamp nasceu em 1453, em Wiltshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, William de Beauchamp, tinha 46 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth de Braybrook, tinha 44 anos. Ele casou-se com Ann Cartwright. Ele faleceu em 12 de junho de 1508, em Espanha, com 55 anos, e foi sepultado em Ludgate, London, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Baron Richard Beauchamp родился в 1453 г. в местности уилцхир, Англия, его отцу по имени William de Beauchamp было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth de Braybrook, 44. Он женился на женщине по имени Ann Cartwright. Он умер 12 июня 1508 г. в местности Испания в возрасте 55 лет и был похоронен в местности луджэит, Лондон, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Baron Richard Beauchamp nació en 1453, en Wiltshire, Inglaterra, su papá, William de Beauchamp, tenía 46 años y su mamá, Elizabeth de Braybrook, tenía 44 años. Se casó con Ann Cartwright. Murió el 12 de junio de 1508, en España, a la edad de 55 años, y fue sepultado en Ludgate, London, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
